title: Parsing of numbers and dates should specify a culture
messageFormat: Pass an explicit IFormatProvider so this parse does not depend on the ambient culture.
summary: The culture-free overloads of Parse and TryParse on double, float, decimal, DateTime and DateTimeOffset read the thread's current culture. The same string then parses differently on two machines: a decimal comma, a date order, or a grouping separator silently changes the result or fails outright.
State the intent explicitly. Use CultureInfo.InvariantCulture for machine-readable data, or the specific user culture when the value really is locale dependent.
